description: Send-capable tool classification read by pre:outbound-send-gate. `deny` (and no `allow` match) = a live SEND. `draft` = a draft tool whose output a human then sends — GATED for a per-recipient approval token (v1.1.0). A HubSpot manage_crm_objects call is gated ONLY when its payload is an OUTBOUND email engagement (detected in code); tasks/notes/deals/reads pass. Patterns are JavaScript regular expressions, anchored ^...$ at match time. Built-in defaults in scripts/lib/outbound-review.js apply when this file is missing or unreadable (fail-closed).
notes: v1.1.0: create_draft moved from `allow` to `draft` — a draft now requires an escc review/approval before it can be created, closing the gap where unreviewed drafts bypassed the gate. HubSpot tools remain allow-listed here; an OUTBOUND email engagement written via manage_crm_objects is detected and gated in code (isHubspotOutboundEmail), while tasks/notes/deals/reads are never blocked. Gmail has no live-send tool (draft-only by construction).
